title: Telo debug event
description: One event in the Telo debug wire format. A kernel runtime (Node, Rust, Go) serializes each emitted event to this shape; it is delivered one-per-line over SSE (`/events`) and JSONL (`/events.jsonl`). Producers reduce payload values to wire-safe forms first: a byte buffer (any file kind) is offloaded to a content-addressed blob store and becomes a `WireBlob` pointer (bytes fetched on demand from `/blobs/<id>`); a resolved `!ref` becomes `{ kind, name }`; any other live or unrepresentable value (controller instance, stream, client, function, bigint) becomes a `"[Marker]"` string; a reference cycle becomes `"[Circular]"`; everything else stays plain JSON.

WireBlob.title: Blob pointer
WireBlob.description: A binary value offloaded from the payload. `$blob` is a path relative to the producer origin; the consumer fetches it from `/blobs/<id>`. The object key the WireBlob sits under is preserved.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 time the producer emitted the event.

payload.description: Event payload, already reduced to wire-safe values. No fixed shape.

metadata.description: Optional producer-attached metadata (namespace, resource, kind, name, …).
